article.body_preview:
DHAKA, May 18 (Reuters) - Apparel makers in Bangladesh are considering rare bulk exports by air to Europe and the United States, as they race to meet deadlines and avoid cancellations after a cyclone delayed shipments, caused power cuts and disrupted production.

The crunch could disrupt summer clothes supplies for retailers such as Walmart (WMT.N), Gap Inc (GPS.N), H&M (HMb.ST), VF Corp (VFC.N), Zara and American Eagle Outfitters (AEO.N), some of Bangladesh's largest export customers.

Cyclone

llm_connections.evidence_quote: has already been hit by weakening global demand, with exports falling in both March and April.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article reports country-wide exports fell in March and April due to weakening global demand; national exports are the export component of the balance of trade.
